Title: BAF60/SWP73 subunits define subclasses of SWI/SNF chromatin remodelling complexes in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SWI/SNF chromatin-remodelling complex subunit; BAF60/SWP73 family
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: indole-3-acetic acid; salicylic acid; raffinose; 4-methoxy-3-indolylmethylglucosinolate; gibberellin-related metabolites
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: auxin; phenolic hormone; raffinose-family oligosaccharide; glucosinolate; diterpenoid hormone
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Auxin controls root development; salicylic acid mediates defence signalling; raffinose contributes to carbohydrate/stress metabolism; indole glucosinolates contribute to defence; gibberellins regulate germination and growth
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Aerial tissues for hormone/metabolite profiling; seeds during germination context; roots for auxin-response phenotypes
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in embryo cells of dry and 24 h imbibed seeds; present in root tip, nascent lateral root primordia, lateral root tips and root hairs
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root meristem zone, lateral root primordia, lateral roots, root hairs; dry and 24 h imbibed seeds
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Auxin treatment (IAA, 2,4-D); germination/imbibition; day-night cycle; sucrose-free medium; salicylic acid treatment
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SWP73B; BRM; SWI3C; BRD1; BRD2; BRD13; BRIP2; ARP4; ARP7; BCL7B
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SWP73A fine-tunes auxin homeostasis and lateral root formation, represses GA3OX1/GA3OX2 during germination, and directly modulates raffinose and indole glucosinolate metabolism; loss of SWP73A slightly lowers IAA, accelerates germination, elevates RS6 and IGMT1 expression, and alters metabolite profiles
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (swp73a); complementation with pSWP73A::SWP73A-GFP; ChIP-qPCR; reanalysis of ChIP-seq; RT-qPCR; metabolite and hormone profiling; confocal microscopy; germination assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SWP73A defines the BAS-A SWI/SNF subclass and shows gene-body-biased chromatin occupancy. 2. SWP73A specifically contributes to auxin-responsive root development and auxin homeostasis, with direct targeting of PIN1, PIN4, PIN7, GH3.17 and YDK1. 3. SWP73A represses GA3OX1 and GA3OX2 during germination, thereby slowing germination dynamics. 4. SWP73A directly regulates RS6 and IGMT1, linking chromatin remodelling to raffinose and glucosinolate metabolism. 5. SWP73A also contributes to SA pathway regulation, including MES9 gene-body occupancy.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: swp73a causes accelerated germination, altered auxin response and altered primary metabolite/amino acid profiles; possible stress-related metabolic shifts
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering germination speed, root architecture and specialized metabolism, but pleiotropic hormone and metabolic effects require caution
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions are chromatin-regulatory rather than catalytic. SWP73A effects are partly masked by SWP73B redundancy but become clearer in swp73b background. Authors propose SWP73A as a specific BAS-A subunit not replaceable for some auxin/germination/metabolic functions.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GSE261097; proteomics (LC-MS/MS): PXD056187; metabolomics by GC-MS and LC-MS; reanalysed ChIP-seq datasets from published studies
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: SWP73A occupancy correlates with chromatin-state regulation; in swp73a seeds, H3K9me2 decreases and H3K4me3 increases at GA3OX1/GA3OX2. SWP73A was previously suggested to function as an H3K9me2 reader and here acts in chromatin-mediated repression.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SWI/SNF chromatin-remodelling complex subunit; BAF60/SWP73 family
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: salicylic acid; gibberellins; raffinose; cytokinins; glucosinolates
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: phenolic hormone; diterpenoid hormone; raffinose-family oligosaccharide; adenine-derived hormone; glucosinolate
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: SA mediates defence; gibberellins regulate growth and flowering; raffinose contributes to carbohydrate/stress metabolism; cytokinins regulate development; glucosinolates mediate defence
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ubiquitous/functional across seedling tissues inferred from mutant-wide developmental phenotypes; exact tissue specificity not detailed
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 2,4-D; BAP; exogenous GA; salicylic acid treatment; day-night cycle; sucrose-free medium
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SWP73A; BRM; SYD; MINU1; MINU2; SWI3A; SWI3B; SWI3C; SWI3D; BRD1; BRD2; BRD13; BRIP2; ARP4; ARP7; BCL7A; BCL7B; SYS1; SYS2; SYS3; GIF2; LFR; BSH
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SWP73B broadly controls hormone and metabolic homeostasis; loss of SWP73B causes SA and cytokinin overaccumulation, reduced GA biosynthesis, raffinose accumulation, altered glucosinolate metabolism, strong sucrose-response defects and severe developmental phenotypes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (swp73b); ChIP-qPCR; reanalysis of ChIP-seq; RT-qPCR; hormone/metabolite profiling; histone mark ChIP-qPCR; exogenous hormone response assays; proteomics
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SWP73B defines BAS-B and is also required in SAS and MAS SWI/SNF subclasses, with promoter/TSS-biased occupancy. 2. SWP73B directly regulates SA biosynthesis genes ICS1, ICS2 and MES9 and represses their expression via chromatin mechanisms. 3. SWP73B directly controls multiple GA pathway genes, and its loss lowers GA12, GA24 and abolishes GA19 while altering GA2OX/GA20OX/GA3OX expression. 4. SWP73B directly regulates RS5 and RS6 and influences raffinose accumulation. 5. SWP73B broadly affects cytokinin, auxin response and glucosinolate-related metabolism.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes dwarfism, male sterility, altered flowering, pin-like organs, strong hormone imbalance and severe growth retardation, especially without sucrose
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a systems-level chromatin target for manipulating hormone-metabolism integration, but severe pleiotropy and developmental penalties make direct deployment risky
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SWP73B has broader and more severe effects than SWP73A because it participates in BAS-B, SAS and MAS complexes. It can function as a repressive factor on active chromatin regions marked by H3K9Ac.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GSE261097; proteomics (LC-MS/MS): PXD056187; metabolomics by GC-MS and LC-MS; reanalysed ChIP-seq datasets from published studies
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Loss of SWP73B increases H3K9Ac/H3K14Ac at ICS1/ICS2 and changes H3K9me2/H3 acetylation at GA loci; SWP73B is required for SWI3B occupancy at several SA and GA biosynthesis promoters and acts in chromatin-based repression.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts chorismate to isochorismate as the main entry step to SA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for major SA biosynthesis route
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Night-end expression changed in swp73 mutants; upregulated in swp73b and downregulated in swp73a at end of night
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elevated ICS1 expression in swp73b associates with SA overaccumulation; reduced ICS1 in swp73a associates with altered SA homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR for SWP73B and SWI3B occupancy; SA metabolite profiling; histone mark ChIP-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ICS1 expression is downregulated in swp73a at night end but upregulated in swp73b. 2. SWP73B directly binds the ICS1 promoter. 3. Loss of SWP73B increases activating histone acetylation at ICS1 and correlates with SA overaccumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper focuses on chromatin regulators controlling ICS1 rather than biochemical characterization of ICS1 itself.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Elevated SA is associated with hypersensitivity to SA and likely defence-growth tradeoffs in swp73b
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential indirect leverage point for tuning SA via chromatin regulators, though direct breeding value of ICS1 is not the paper’s main focus
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: SWP73B-dependent chromatin repression; increased H3K9Ac and H3K14Ac at promoter in swp73b

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts chorismate to isochorismate in the SA biosynthetic route
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Down in swp73a and swp73b at night end; strongly upregulated 6 h after dawn in swp73b; SA treatment causes downregulation, especially in swp73b
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Misregulation of ICS2 in swp73b contributes to altered SA biosynthesis/perception dynamics
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ICS2 expression is dynamically altered in both mutants, most strongly in swp73b. 2. SWP73B directly binds ICS2. 3. SA treatment causes especially strong ICS2 downregulation in swp73b, indicating altered feedback/perception.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Prominent in feedback regulation context of SA homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Misregulation linked to SA imbalance and growth inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: In swp73b, increased H3K9Ac at TSS correlates with elevated expression at selected timepoints

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts methyl salicylate to salicylic acid
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in swp73b at night end; downregulated in swp73a
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MES9 upregulation in swp73b and downregulation in swp73a indicate differential SWP73 control of SA regeneration/homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MES9 is strongly induced in swp73b and reduced in swp73a. 2. SWP73B binds the MES9 promoter/5' region and SWP73A binds the MES9 gene body. 3. Differential control of MES9 supports distinct BAS-A and SWP73B-containing complex functions in SA feedback homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Grouped with MES2 in expression analysis, but direct occupancy shown for MES9
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: SWI3B occupancy at MES9 depends on SWP73B

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 2-oxidation of gibberellins to inactive forms
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: swp73b alters GA catabolic gene expression, contributing to overall GA imbalance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA2OX1 expression is reduced in swp73b. 2. SWP73B directly binds its proximal promoter. 3. SWI3B occupancy at the promoter depends on SWP73B.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although catabolic, treated as pathway gene due to direct GA metabolic role.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Contributes to growth defects via GA imbalance

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 2-oxidation of gibberellins to inactive forms
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Misregulation contributes to reduced GA homeostasis in swp73b
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA2OX2 is upregulated in swp73b. 2. SWP73B directly binds the 5'UTR/TSS region. 3. SWI3B occupancy depends on SWP73B.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Growth defects associated with GA imbalance

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative conversion steps in late GA biosynthesis toward bioactive GA precursors
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step inferred for late GA pathway, not explicitly established here
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ~6-fold elevated in swp73b at middle of day
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Aberrant GA20OX2 induction is part of broader SWP73B-mediated rewiring of GA pathway genes in swp73b
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA20OX2 is strongly induced in swp73b at midday. 2. SWP73B binds its proximal promoter. 3. Increased H3K9Ac and H3K14Ac at the promoter correlate with upregulation in swp73b.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite upregulation, overall bioactive GA pathway remains deficient in swp73b due to broader pathway disruption.
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Increased H3K9Ac and H3K14Ac at promoter in swp73b

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 3-beta hydroxylation steps crucial for synthesis of bioactive gibberellins
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for bioactive GA formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in swp73b; peaks faster during germination in swp73a before radicle emergence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Naturally peaks during stratification/imbibition according to cited public database analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SWP73A represses GA3OX1 during germination, slowing germination; loss of SWP73B lowers GA pathway output and alters growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR during germination and in seedlings; ChIP-qPCR in dry/imbibed seeds and seedlings; germination assays; histone mark ChIP-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA3OX1 is a direct chromatin target of SWP73A during germination and of SWP73B in seedlings. 2. In swp73a, GA3OX1 expression rises earlier, correlating with accelerated germination. 3. In swp73b, GA3OX1 expression is reduced as part of impaired GA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Central mechanistic link between SWP73A and germination dynamics.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changing GA3OX1 timing affects seed germination behavior and growth
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant target for manipulating germination speed, potentially via chromatin regulators rather than direct enzyme modification
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pSWP73A native promoter in complementation line
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: In swp73a seeds, reduced H3K9me2 and increased H3K4me3 at GA3OX1 accompany derepression

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 3-beta hydroxylation required for bioactive gibberellin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for bioactive GA formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in swp73b; peaks earlier in swp73a during germination
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Derepression of GA3OX2 in swp73a accelerates germination; reduced expression in swp73b contributes to GA deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR; germination assays; histone mark ChIP-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SWP73A occupies GA3OX2 in imbibed seeds and represses its early expression. 2. swp73a exhibits earlier GA3OX2 induction and faster germination. 3. SWP73B also binds the GA3OX2 promoter region in seedlings, and its mutation reduces SWI3B occupancy and disrupts GA pathway regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Major shared node between SWP73A germination control and SWP73B vegetative GA control.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering expression changes germination kinetics and growth
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential route to tune germination speed
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pSWP73A native promoter in complementation line
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: In swp73a seeds, H3K9me2 decreases and H3K4me3 increases at GA3OX2

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in swp73b; not activated during imbibition and not altered in swp73a germination assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not activated during imbibition according to paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Part of SWP73-regulated GA biosynthesis network, though less central to germination than GA3OX1/2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SWP73B binds the GA3OX3 promoter and SWP73A binds the gene body. 2. swp73b reduces GA3OX3 expression. 3. GA3OX3 is not induced during imbibition, distinguishing it from GA3OX1/2 in germination control.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Less decisive than GA3OX1/2 for the seed-germination phenotype.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactosyl moiety from galactinol to sucrose to form raffinose
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SWP73B directly promotes/represses proper RS5 expression control; misregulation associates with raffinose accumulation in swp73b
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RS5 expression is elevated in swp73b. 2. SWP73B binds the RS5 promoter. 3. SWP73B-containing complexes contribute to raffinose homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct target of SWP73B, not SWP73A, in presented data.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Raffinose accumulation indicates altered carbon partitioning
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential handle for raffinose engineering, but upstream chromatin control is pleiotropic

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose from galactinol to sucrose to produce raffinose
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in swp73a at end of night and constitutively in swp73b
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Dark inducible (as stated by alias DIN10)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct misregulation of RS6 by both SWP73 proteins correlates with raffinose accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RS6 is directly controlled by both SWP73A and SWP73B. 2. SWP73B binds the promoter whereas SWP73A binds the gene body. 3. RS6 is upregulated in swp73a and swp73b, linking both BAS-A and SWP73B-containing complexes to raffinose homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents a clear example of differential promoter vs gene-body control by SWP73B vs SWP73A.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Raffinose overaccumulation may alter carbon allocation and stress metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering raffinose levels, though regulator manipulation may cause broad pleiotropy

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: O-methylates indole glucosinolate intermediates to generate methoxylated indole glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seedling aerial tissues sampled at end of day
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in swp73a at end of day
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: End of day; SA-related defence context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SWP73A directly represses IGMT1-associated methoxylated indole glucosinolate accumulation; swp73a accumulates 4mIMG
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. swp73a accumulates 4-methoxy-3-indolylmethylglucosinolate. 2. IGMT1 is upregulated in swp73a. 3. SWP73A binds the IGMT1 gene body, indicating direct chromatin-level control of indole glucosinolate metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supports a specific metabolic role for BAS-A complexes in specialized defence metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changes in glucosinolate profile may affect defence-growth balance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential route to modulate indole glucosinolate composition
